entries.rationale: Need deterministic pure-Python accounting without framework runtime coupling.
entries.boundary: Local accounting core only; consumed by local portfolio APIs; no upstream imports.

entries.rationale: Keep local type contracts aligned with proven semantics while preserving HB-native wiring.
entries.boundary: Only contract-level adaptation; local dataclasses and enums remain repo-owned.

entries.upstream_component: maker/taker and fixed commission model concepts
entries.rationale: Reuse proven fee-model semantics, but keep connector/profile integration local.
entries.boundary: Local fee model protocol + factory; no upstream class dependency at runtime.

entries.upstream_component: FillModelConfig conventions and depth/queue behavior families
entries.rationale: Borrow model taxonomy for determinism while retaining local fill implementation controls.
entries.boundary: All fill logic implemented locally behind local FillModel protocol.

entries.rationale: Preserve familiar config semantics while keeping local runtime and serialization boundaries.
entries.boundary: Local LatencyModel dataclass only; no dependency on external runtime objects.

entries.rationale: Funding cadence behavior is reused conceptually, with local accounting/event contracts.
entries.boundary: Local simulator and portfolio calls only; no upstream module import.

entries.upstream_component: risk engine architecture and margin ladder concepts
entries.rationale: Reuse proven liquidation-level design while keeping desk-specific constraints configurable.
entries.boundary: Local risk hooks and decisions; no upstream risk-engine classes imported.

entries.rationale: Keep separation of price PnL, fees, and margin semantics while retaining desk invariants.
entries.boundary: Delegates to local accounting core and local ledger/risk engines only.

entries.rationale: Retain exchange-realistic matching controls while preserving local execution contracts.
entries.boundary: Local engine orchestration around local fill/fee/portfolio interfaces.
